tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
## 📝 Blog Topic Ideas - April 16, 2026

### **1. Agentic AI Cost Optimization: The First-Class Architectural Concern**
**ANGLE:** Move beyond "AI is cheaper" to concrete cost models for agentic systems. This topic analyzes how Anthropic's 2026 report treats agent cost optimization as a first-class architectural concern, similar to cloud cost optimization. I'll cover real trade-offs: when agents save money vs. when they burn credits faster than human labor, drawing from my $100K+ annual cloud savings experience.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Anthropic's 2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report (March 2026) explicitly states "treating agent cost optimization as a first-class architectural concern." [Read the report](https://resources.anthropic.com/2026-agentic-coding-trends-report). MachineLearningMastery.com notes this trend with DeepSeek R1 models running at fractions of typical costs. LinkedIn article on April 2026 shift from chat to execution highlights implementation patterns.

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

### **2. AMD Strix Halo Local LLM Inference: A Practical Guide to ROCm Stability**
**ANGLE:** Hands-on guide for running local LLMs on AMD hardware without the NVIDIA tax. I'll cover specific kernel versions (6.18.4+), ROCm versions (7.2+), and firmware fixes that resolved Strix Halo instability in January-March 2026, including benchmark data comparing to equivalent NVIDIA setups. This draws directly from my AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server experience.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Reddit r/ROCm thread from January 2026 details Linux kernel 6.18.4+ and ROCm 7.2+ stability fixes for gfx1151 architecture. [View the discussion](https://www.reddit.com/r/ROCm/comments/1qhm21v/rocmlinux_on_amd_strix_halo_january_2026_stable/). Medium article "What to Buy for Local LLMs (April 2026)" by Julien Simon discusses AMD mini PCs as complete systems. William Male's blog on Ollama + AMD GPU setup provides practical troubleshooting steps.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

### **3. MCP at Enterprise Scale: From 10K Servers to Production Deployment Patterns**
**ANGLE:** Transition from experimentation to enterprise-wide MCP adoption in 2026. I'll analyze the journey from pilot to production, covering obstacles early adopters faced (governance checkpoints, integration with existing systems), and include practical deployment patterns for connecting AI agents to internal data sources and tools. This builds on my experience building two production multi-agent systems.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** CData Software reports "Movement from pilots to production: 2026 marks the transition." March 14th 2026 article in The New Stack notes MCP crossed significant milestones. Data shows 10,000+ active public MCP servers and 97M monthly SDK downloads by March 2026. Linux Foundation governance confirmed.

**SCORE:** 9/10

---

### **4. DORA Metrics in the Age of AI: Attribution Layers Beyond Aggregate Dashboards**
**ANGLE:** Why traditional DORA metrics fail when measuring AI-assisted work. I'll discuss how elite teams now need attribution layers to separate AI-assisted from human-authored work, including practical frameworks for implementing these attribution systems without adding bureaucracy. This addresses gaps in my own SLO management and engineering culture experience.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Oobeya.io article "DORA Metrics Are Not Enough in 2026" (March 2026) explains the need for attribution layers. [Read the analysis](https://www.oobeya.io/blog/dora-metrics-not-enough-2026). Gitrecap Blog details how blameless retrospectives must evolve with AI assistance. Engineering Manager Tools framework updated for 2026 covers both traditional metrics and AI-specific measurements.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

### **5. Building Trust in Remote Engineering Teams: From Presence to Output Models**
**ANGLE:** Practical systems for building trust beyond virtual coffee chats. I'll discuss how high-trust cultures form in distributed teams through structured output-based accountability rather than presence monitoring, including specific patterns for async communication, time zone management, and vulnerability-based trust development. This draws from my experience leading up to 14 cross-functional members.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Monday.com Blog "Building High-Trust Culture" (March 2026) discusses team trust reducing manual checks. Medium article by Kılıç on March 16th 2026 emphasizes stopping confusion between green dots and alignment. [Read the article](https://medium.com/@khanmumtaz1/why-trust-is-the-ultimate-remote-leadership-skill-abdcdacb202a). Remote Engineering Team Management guide from EM Tools updated March 30th 2026 covers distributed culture strategies.

**SCORE:** 7/10

---

### **6. The Engineering Leadership Blind Spot: AI Visibility vs. Efficiency Theater**
**ANGLE:** Why the strategic question in 2026 isn't "whether to adopt AI" but "how to build organizational visibility." I'll discuss how leaders often implement AI for efficiency theater rather than extracting full value across delivery cycles, including frameworks for measuring real AI impact on engineering outcomes. This addresses my experience driving 28% to 100% department-wide AI adoption.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Waydev.co article "More Code, Fewer Releases: The Engineering Leadership Blind Spot of 2026" (April 2026) directly addresses this gap. [Read the analysis](https://waydev.co/engineering-leadership-blind-spot-of-2026/). Datasociety.com reports on how 2026 divides organizations treating AI as quick wins vs. building real capability.

**SCORE:** 8/10

---

### **7. Learning in the Flow of Work: Engineering Leadership Continuous Development Systems**
**ANGLE:** How top engineering leaders embed continuous learning directly into daily tasks rather than relying on annual training cycles. I'll discuss practical systems for "Learning in the Flow of Work" including AI-powered check-ins, simulation-based judgment building, and outcomes-led development frameworks. This draws from my experience mentoring 5 engineers to team lead roles.

**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Electives.io lists "10 learning trends to watch in 2026" highlighting fluency, judgment, and collaboration over basic prompt engineering. TrainingIndustry.com reports on outcomes-led learning becoming standard practice with tools like Slack/Teams integration. JRG Partners data shows continuous learning as key differentiator for top leaders.

**SCORE:** 7/10

---

### 📊 Summary Table

| # | Topic Title | Pillar | Score |
|---|-------------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Agentic AI Cost Optimization | Technology | **9/10** |
| 2 | AMD Strix Halo Local LLM Inference | Technology | **8/10** |
| 3 | MCP at Enterprise Scale | Technology | **9/10** |
| 4 | DORA Metrics in the Age of AI | Technology | **8/10** |
| 5 | Building Trust in Remote Engineering Teams | Leadership | **7/10** |
| 6 | The Engineering Leadership Blind Spot | Leadership | **8/10** |
| 7 | Learning in the Flow of Work | Life | **7/10** |

### ✅ Next Steps
- All topics saved to `/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-16.md`
- Push notification sent with titles and scores
- Ready to draft any topic on demand (800-1200 words each)
